4 min read
Just now

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

Modern applications are moving from traditional request-response patterns toward real-time experiences. AI chat interfaces, live dashboards, upload progress indicators, notifications, and collaborative tools all share one common requirement:
Data arrives gradually, and the UI needs to update immediately.
In Angular, signals have already changed how we think about state management. With the introduction of the stream capability in resource(), asynchronous data is no longer limited to a single resolved value.
Instead of:
Request → Wait → Receive complete response → Renderwe can now build:
Request → Receive chunks → Update signal → Render continuouslyThis creates a much more natural model for streaming experiences.
The Problem With Traditional Async Patterns
Before streaming resources, developers usually handled incremental data using RxJS.
A typical implementation required:

• Creating a Subject

• Listening to incoming chunks

• Accumulating values using…
